methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Humanoid, a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Artem Sokolov, has raised $50M to date. The broader sector includes nine private robotics companies (1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics among them) and one public comparable, though no peers share identical stage, funding profile, or valuation tier in sufficient quantity to support reliable median benchmarking.

This analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement. Using the $50M total raised as a valuation proxy yields an implied value of $125M. This method is applicable given the company's clear seed classification, though it assumes Humanoid's risk-return profile aligns with sector norms for stage advancement.

The triangulation results in a median valuation of $125M, with a plausible range of $106M to $144M reflecting ±15% sensitivity around the single-method estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable-company validation (peer-median method inapplicable due to insufficient same/adjacent-stage peers), product-market traction data, and capital efficiency metrics relative to the broader robotics cohort. Revenue, burn rate, and customer pipeline visibility would materially inform confidence in this range.
